summary: Russian tactical aviation and UAV activity intensified across multiple fronts, striking civilian areas in Kramatorsk, Zaporizhzhia, Kyiv and Odesa. Ukraine reported significant civilian casualties from drone and missile attacks and is reinforcing perimeter defenses around Odesa. Recruitment of foreign fighters by Russia continues, with thousands already killed.
key_points: Russian tactical aviation increased activity on the south‑east front, threatening near‑front regions (Source: @kpszsu/62843).
Two Russian aviation bombs hit Kramatorsk centre, damaging multi‑storey buildings and injuring at least five civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/81457).
Russian forces launched a missile strike on Zaporizhzhia, wounding two civilians and a police officer and damaging nearby infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81471).
Ukraine’s Ministry of Internal Affairs reported 70‑100 civilian deaths weekly from drone, ballistic, cruise missile and aerial bomb attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81472).
Odesa is preparing a perimeter defence with kilometres of anti‑tank ditches, dugouts and barbed wire installations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30440).
Russia has recruited over 28,000 foreign citizens, with at least 5,000 killed in the war, and plans to recruit an additional 18,500 in 2026 (Source: @UkraineNow/81455).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV movements across the northern front, including mesh‑drone leading strike UAVs and multiple relay UAVs in Chernihiv and Sumy regions (Source: @eRadarrua/93729).

sections.executive_summary: Russian tactical aviation intensified, striking Kramatorsk and Zaporizhzhia, while Ukraine reports high civilian casualties from aerial attacks. Odesa is fortifying its perimeter defenses against possible ground assaults. Russia continues large‑scale recruitment of foreign fighters, sustaining manpower for the conflict.

points: The Ukrainian Ministry of Internal Affairs stated that 70‑100 civilians are killed each week due to attacks by drones, ballistic missiles, cruise missiles and aerial bombs, highlighting a severe civilian toll (Source: @UkraineNow/81472).
In Kramatorsk, two Russian aviation bombs were dropped on the city centre, damaging numerous high‑rise buildings and vehicles; rescue operations are ongoing (Source: @UkraineNow/81457).
A missile strike on Zaporizhzhia injured two civilians and a police officer, and caused damage to transport and nearby structures (Source: @UkraineNow/81471).

Ukraine completed emergency repair work in Kyiv’s Shevchenkivskyi district after a Russian strike on a residential building killed two people, injured 92 (including three children), and damaged a five‑storey block (Source: @UkraineNow/81458).
Odesa is constructing kilometres of anti‑tank ditches, dugouts and barbed‑wire barriers as part of a perimeter defence plan (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30440).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV activity on the northern front, including mesh‑drone leading strike UAVs and relay UAVs over Chernihiv and Sumy regions (Source: @eRadarrua/93729).

On the Sumy front, Ukrainian “North” Group units advanced up to 700 m on twenty‑two sectors, with ongoing skirmishes in Zapselye, Ivolzhansk, Kondratovka and surrounding settlements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30430).
Russian tactical aviation showed heightened activity on the south‑east direction, posing a threat to near‑front areas (Source: @kpszsu/62843).
Russia announced systematic bombing of Kyiv, though the night was reportedly calmer for UAV strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30430).
Russian forces claimed control over the villages of Ryasny and Zapsel in Sumy region (Source: @tass_agency/377609).
Russia plans to recruit an additional 18,500 foreign fighters in 2026, adding to the 28,000 already recruited, of whom at least 5,000 have been killed (Source: @UkraineNow/81455).
A Russian Iskander missile struck the UN World Food Programme warehouse in Dnipro, destroying food supplies for 130,000 people valued at $1.4 million (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).
Russian Foreign Ministry announced the start of systematic bombing of Kyiv, indicating an escalation in air operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30430).
Russian tactical aviation activity was also reported on the north‑east front, threatening front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/62849).

Ukrainian “North” Group forces advanced up to 700 m on twenty‑two sectors of the Sumy front, with skirmishes in Zapselye, Ivolzhansk, Kondratovka, Pisarevka and nearby settlements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30430).
UAVs were observed moving from Belgorod region southward into Sumy region (Source: @kpszsu/62855).
Multiple relay and strike UAVs were reported over Chernihiv oblast, including routes from Komyshuvata to Zaporizhzhia and from Snovsk toward Sedniv/Berezna (Source: @eRadarrua/93738, @kpszsu/62831).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the north‑east direction, increasing threat to front‑line zones (Source: @kpszsu/62849).
Ukrainian forces reported mesh‑drone leading strike UAVs with subsequent relay UAVs in the area (Source: @eRadarrua/93729).
UAV activity reported in Chernihiv region: mesh‑drone leading strike UAVs, relay UAVs near Snovsk, and reactive UAVs near Slavutych water reservoir (Source: @eRadarrua/93729, @eRadarrua/93739).
B‑P LA observed moving from the north‑west towards Sosnytsia in Chernihiv oblast (Source: @kpszsu/62826).
New reactive UAVs were sighted heading toward Slavutych and Snovsk in northern Chernihiv (Source: @eRadarrua/93740).
UAVs were reported heading toward Vilnyansky from the south‑east in Zaporizhzhia region, indicating cross‑front activity (Source: @kpszsu/62830).
Ukrainian forces noted a mesh‑drone leading a strike UAV, suggesting coordinated UAV tactics on the northern front (Source: @eRadarrua/93729).
Odesa is fortifying its perimeter with anti‑tank ditches, dugouts and barbed wire as part of a defensive preparation (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30440).
Russian tactical aviation activity increased on the south‑east direction, threatening southern front areas (Source: @kpszsu/62843).
A new UAV group from Kherson region is moving toward Mykolaiv, targeting Snihurivka (Source: @kpszsu/62842).
Russian missile strike on the UN WFP warehouse in Dnipro destroyed food supplies for 130,000 people (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV movements from the south‑east of Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @kpszsu/62807).
Russian tactical aviation showed heightened activity on the south‑east and north‑east fronts, posing threats to near‑front regions (Source: @kpszsu/62843, @kpszsu/62849).
Two Russian aviation bombs struck Kramatorsk centre, causing civilian injuries and infrastructure damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81457).
A missile strike hit Zaporizhzhia, wounding civilians and damaging transport assets (Source: @UkraineNow/81471).
Ukrainian forces reported mesh‑drone leading strike UAVs with subsequent relay UAVs, indicating coordinated UAV tactics (Source: @eRadarrua/93729).
Russian Foreign Ministry announced systematic bombing of Kyiv, though UAV activity remained comparatively calmer (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30430).
Ukrainian air defence missile shortages were reported, with Kyiv requesting 5‑10 missiles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30430).

key_developments: Russian tactical aviation intensified on the south‑east front, threatening near‑front areas (Source: @kpszsu/62843).
Two Russian aviation bombs hit Kramatorsk centre, damaging multi‑storey buildings and injuring civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/81457).
A missile strike on Zaporizhzhia wounded two civilians and a police officer, damaging nearby infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81471).
Ukraine reports 70‑100 civilian deaths weekly from drone, ballistic, cruise missile and aerial bomb attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81472).
Odesa is constructing kilometres of anti‑tank ditches, dugouts and barbed‑wire barriers for perimeter defence (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30440).
Russia has recruited over 28,000 foreign citizens, with at least 5,000 killed, and plans to recruit an additional 18,500 in 2026 (Source: @UkraineNow/81455).
Ukrainian UAV operations on the northern front involve mesh‑drone leading strike UAVs and relay UAVs across Chernihiv and Sumy regions (Source: @eRadarrua/93729).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian aerial activity, including tactical aviation and UAV operations, is escalating across multiple fronts, increasing pressure on civilian populations and Ukrainian defensive positions. The recruitment drive for foreign fighters suggests Russia is seeking to sustain its manpower despite significant casualties. Ukraine's fortification efforts around Odesa and reported advances on the Sumy front indicate a mixed defensive posture, but civilian casualty rates remain high, underscoring the conflict's intensity.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine's ministries are highlighting civilian losses and urging international support, while reinforcing key cities such as Odesa with perimeter defenses. International actors, including Estonia, have voiced opposition to mediation, and China has called for restraint, reflecting diplomatic tension. Russian statements about systematic bombing of Kyiv and expanded foreign recruitment signal a continued commitment to offensive operations.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths in Chernihiv and Sumy regions reveals coordinated mesh‑drone tactics that could precede larger strike formations. The reported shortage of air‑defence missiles in Kyiv may affect the city's ability to repel future aerial attacks. The UN WFP warehouse strike in Dnipro demonstrates Russia's targeting of humanitarian infrastructure, potentially impacting civilian supply lines near the front.
